content.claim: Hungarian mainstream classrooms typically comprise 24 children with one teacher for all subjects and one specialist monitoring progress across four years, lacking the dual-teacher and dedicated special education teacher (SET) co-teaching infrastructure central to Finland's tiered support model implementation
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Hungarian baseline staffing ratios cited but source document not directly accessible
No quantification of effort required to reach Finnish dual-staffing levels in Hungarian context
Regional variation in Hungarian class sizes and specialist allocation not specified
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
